Here is a self-contained handoff prompt. Paste it as the first message of a fresh Claude Code session started in /Users/brien/Workspaces.  ``` Pick up intake issue theparlor/intake#28: an omnibus 19-section renovation-program capture from a 3-hour drive (Fri Jul 10 2026). It is labeled route:filing + host-pending and is waiting for a full session to file and organize. Read the issue body verbatim first:    gh issue view 28 --repo theparlor/intake --json title,body,labels  GOAL Examine the captured material, then file and contextualize it into my project space so it stops being one wall-of-text issue and becomes actionable, correctly-placed workstream artifacts wired into the knowledge graph.  BEFORE WRITING ANYTHING 1. Read the placement resolver at /Users/brien/Workspaces/AGENTS.md, then apply the    governance chain: /Users/brien/Workspaces/Core/products/digital-declutter/RULES.md Rule 25    walk (AGENTS.md -> RULES.md -> references/workspaces-structure.md -> context-md-template.md). 2. Read the owning hub and its current state so you extend, not duplicate:    /Users/brien/Workspaces/Home/TheParlor/Renovation/CONTEXT.md (master index)    /Users/brien/Workspaces/Home/TheParlor/Renovation/decisions-and-open-questions.md    and the existing subfolders (Tools/, Stripping/, FloorInlay/, Design/). 3. This is Home/, not Core/ or Work/. No engagement scope, no NDA boundary. Reversible local    filing is L4: execute and signal, do not ask permission per file.  WHAT TO PRODUCE - Resequence the 19 sections into per-workstream artifacts under the Renovation hub. Candidate   workstreams from the capture: drainage/perc/French-drain/dry-well, masonry (can well, patio,   grill pad, repointing), deck+railing+spiral-stair (with the MA 780 CMR code notes), gutters,   fascia/trim, kitchen door, library paint-cure failure. Group by trade where the capture already   does (section 14 is the trades stack: Alfonso masonry, James electrical, plumber, chimney 2-trade). - The capture asks for a 3-doc GC packet per trade: scope of work, bill of materials,   sequence+schedule vs the Oct 15 deadline. Stub those where the material supports it. - Extract the resolved DECISION atoms into decisions-and-open-questions.md: can-well no-drain   (sealed sump basin), dry-well-plus-pump (two decoupled systems), slider-as-pocket for the   kitchen door with a styled well-insulated fixed panel. Keep the open threads open (deck material   shared decision with Elisha, French-door aesthetic, 42-inch guard confirmation). - Update every CONTEXT.md you touch. Zero orphaned files. YAML frontmatter (title, type, maturity,   created) on every new markdown doc. Lowercase-hyphenated filenames.  ACUTE, DO THIS FIRST Section 16 has a hard deadline independent of the Oct 15 program: black Azek Captivate "Midnight Black" fascia must be in hand by end of Saturday for a Monday 3-window install. National Lumber Newton (15 Needham St) is closest, short Saturday hours (~8-1), closed Sunday. Surface this as the top action with the call-National-Lumber-Friday-morning step before you do any of the slower filing work. Do not let it get buried under the resequencing.  DO NOT - Commit or push until placement is verified (specific-file staging only, never git add -A). - Send anything to another human (no Slack, email, or calls executed on my behalf: L0). - Overwrite any ratified artifact; stage reworks to a sibling filename.  CLOSE-OUT When filing is done, emit a session-signal capturing what was placed and which GC packets remain stubbed, commit the Renovation hub changes with a conventional message, then close #28 with a completion receipt naming the paths written. Leave open threads as tracked open questions, not as done. ```  Two notes on how I scoped it:  - I front-loaded the Saturday fascia deadline because it is the one time-critical item hiding inside a doc that otherwise reads as a leisurely program plan. If the new session works top-to-bottom it would hit that in section 16 with the window already gone. - I pointed it at the governance chain rather than letting it improvise placement, since this is a big multi-workstream capture and the failure mode is dumping 19 sections into one file at the hub root instead of splitting into properly-contextualized workstream folders.  Want me to also pre-stage the empty workstream folders and CONTEXT.md stubs now so the new session starts from scaffolding instead of a blank hub, or leave all of it for that session to structure from scratch?
